Initialize an E2B sandbox.\n2. Run a simple command.\nFor example:\n```\nresult = sandbox.commands.run('ls -l') print(result)\n```\n3. A RuntimeError will raise with the message about accessing\n```\nresponse.content\n```\non a streaming response."},{"type":"text","text":"# e2b-dev/code-interpreter: Python & JS/TS SDK for running ... - GitHub\ncreated: 2024-03-11, last updated: 2025-10-27, [Read more](https://github.com/e2b-dev/code-interpreter)\n\nE2B is an open-source infrastructure that allows you run to AI-generated code in secure isolated sandboxes in the cloud.\nTo start and control sandboxes, use our JavaScript SDK or Python SDK.\n…\n### 3.\nExecute code with code interpreter inside Sandbox\nJavaScript / TypeScript\n```\nimport { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'\nconst sbx = await Sandbox.create()\nawait sbx.runCode('x = 1')\nconst execution = await sbx.runCode('x+=1; x')\nconsole.log(execution.text) // outputs 2\n```\nPython\n```\nfrom e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox\nwith Sandbox.create() as sandbox:\nsandbox.run_code(\"x = 1\")\nexecution = sandbox.run_code(\"x+=1; x\")\nprint(execution.text) # outputs 2\n```\n### 4.\nCheck docs\nVisit E2B documentation.\n### 5."},{"type":"text","text":"# Running E2B Desktop Sandbox\ncreated: 2026-03-11, last updated: 2026-03-16, [Read more](https://openkruise.io/kruiseagents/best-practices/running-e2b-for-desktop)\n\nYou can install the E2B desktop Python SDK with the following command:\n```\npip install e2b-desktop\n```\n…\n```\nimport time\nfrom e2b_desktop import Sandbox\n# Create a new desktop sandbox\ndesktop = Sandbox.create(template=\"desktop\")\nprint(f\"sandboxId: {desktop.sandbox_id}\")\n# Note: There can be only one stream at a time\n# You need to stop the current stream before streaming another application\ndesktop.stream.start(\n# window_id=desktop.get_current_window_id(), # if not provided the whole desktop will be streamed\nrequire_auth=True\n)\n# Get the stream auth key\nauth_key = desktop.stream.get_auth_key()\n# Print the stream URL\nprint('Stream URL:', desktop.stream.get_url(auth_key=auth_key))\ninput(\"press ENTER to exit\")\n# Kill the sandbox after the tasks are finished\ndesktop.kill()\n```\n#### 2.1.2 Controlling Remote Desktop ​\nThe following code demonstrates operations such as opening a browser and entering a URL.\n…\n#### 2.1.3 Other Basics ​\nIn addition to the above desktop features, basic functions such as command execution, file operations, pause/resume, etc.\nare also supported."},{"type":"text","text":"# Sandbox - E2B Docs\ncreated: 2026-01-07, last updated: 2026-03-13, [Read more](https://pypi.org/project/e2b/)\n\n# e2b 2.4.2\npip install e2b\nReleased:\n...\nE2B is an open-source infrastructure that allows you to run AI-generated code in secure isolated sandboxes in the cloud.\nTo start and control sandboxes, use our JavaScript SDK or Python SDK.\n## Run your first Sandbox\n### 1.\nInstall SDK\n```\npip install e2b-code-interpreter\n```\n### 2.\nGet your E2B API key\n```\nE2B_API_KEY=e2b_***\n```\n### 3.\nExecute code with code interpreter inside Sandbox\n```\nfrom e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox\nwith Sandbox.create() as sandbox:\nsandbox.run_code(\"x = 1\")\nexecution = sandbox.run_code(\"x+=1; x\")\nprint(execution.text) # outputs 2\n```"},{"type":"text","text":"# The terminal can't execute the \"cd xxx\" command · Issue #442 · e2b-dev/E2B\ncreated: 2024-09-18, last updated: 2025-07-18, [Read more](https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/issues/442)\n\n**Expected behavior** E2B terminal works as the real terminal.\n**Terminal commands & output**\n```\nEnter command: ls\njava\njdk-11.0.2\njdk-11.0.2_linux-x64_bin.tar.gz\nEnter command: cd java\nEnter command: ls -a\n.\n..\n.bash_logout\n.bashrc\n.profile\n.sudo_as_admin_successful\njava\njdk-11.0.2\njdk-11.0.2_linux-x64_bin.tar.gz\n```\n…\n**If you want just to execute a command, the\n```\nsandbox.process.start_and_wait\n```\n(or\n```\nsandbox.commands.run\n```\nin Beta SDK) might be a better solution here — the terminal/pty is made to be connected to an actual terminal emulator, outputting all the colored output and taking into account things like terminal size, which might not be relevant for you.**"},{"type":"text","text":"# Running E2B Code Interpreter Sandbox - OpenKruise\ncreated: 2026-03-11, last updated: 2026-03-11, [Read more](https://openkruise.io/kruiseagents/best-practices/running-e2b-for-code-interpreter)\n\nYou can install the E2B code interpreter Python SDK with the following command:\n```\npip install e2b-code-interpreter\n```\n### 2.1 E2B Standard Capabilities ​\n`sandbox-manager` is compatible with E2B's standard capabilities, including management and sandbox instance operations.\nThe following example demonstrates creating sandboxes, executing code, operating files, executing commands, and other\nfunctions through the E2B SDK.\n…\n```\nimport os\n# Import the E2B SDK\nfrom e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox\n# Create a sandbox using the E2B Python SDK\n# The template name here must match the SandboxSet name\nsbx = Sandbox.create(template=\"code-interpreter\", timeout=300)\nprint(f\"sandbox id: {sbx.sandbox_id}\")\nsbx.kill()\nprint(f\"sandbox {sbx.sandbox_id} killed\")\n```\n…\n#### 2.1.4 Command Execution ​\n```\nwith Sandbox.create(template=\"code-interpreter\", timeout=300) as sbx:\nresult = sbx.commands.run('echo hello; sleep 1; echo world', on_stdout=lambda data: print(data),\n on_stderr=lambda data: print(data))\nprint(result)\n```"},{"type":"text","text":"# E2B\ncreated: 2026-01-29, last updated: 2026-03-15, [Read more](https://docs.agno.com/tools/toolkits/others/e2b)\n\n**E2BTools** Sandbox ID and API key saved.\")\n\nfinally:\n    # We do NOT call sbx.kill() or sbx.close() to keep it alive for 10 minutes\n    # The timeout=600 ensures it stays alive in the cloud.\n    # However, we should properly close the local connection if needed, \n    # but for a simple script it's fine.\n    pass\n"},"output":{"success":true},"callProviderMetadata":{"google":{"thoughtSignature":""}}},{"type":"step-start"},{"type":"tool-executeCommand","toolCallId":"i6DCJQeBTgtdXPUW","state":"output-available","input":{"command":"export E2B_API_KEY=e2b_d6a5e0547a2b3052ff3c9ba2c7ba3d94ed758b24 && python3 /home/user/solve.py"},"output":{"output":"Starting E2B sandbox...\r\nSandbox spawned with ID: ig2vqhhpjf5cz65htg5xj\r\nExecuting command in sandbox...\r\nSuccess!
